findings.observation: Every patch row displays a CVE identifier (e.g., CVE-2026-0686) as the patch name. Patches and CVEs are different entities. The search placeholder says 'Search patches (KB, USN, RHSA...)' but no patches have those names. Only 1 of 207,222 has a real patch name ('Google Dawn Use-After-Free Vulnerability').
findings.suggestion: Fix the catalog normalization pipeline to populate actual patch identifiers (KB5034441, USN-6543-1, RHSA-2026:0123). CVE IDs belong in the CVE LINKS column, not PATCH NAME.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Status Text Mapping; enterprise.md > No Debug Artifacts

findings.element: CVE LINKS column — all rows show '--'
findings.observation: Every single patch shows em-dash for CVE Links. The CVE-to-patch correlation pipeline is completely unpopulated. This is the core value proposition of a patch management product — knowing which patches fix which vulnerabilities.
findings.suggestion: Verify the CVE-patch correlation job is running and the patch_cves join table is populated. Even for POC, a significant percentage of patches must show linked CVEs.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null; enterprise.md > Data credibility

findings.element: CVSS column — all rows show '--'; detail page shows '0.0/10'
findings.observation: CVSS is empty on list page (--) but detail page shows 0.0/10 for the same data. 0.0 is a valid CVSS score meaning 'no impact' — showing it for missing data is misleading. Detail page 0.0 is colored green (healthy) which contradicts the 'High' severity badge.
findings.suggestion: Show '--' or 'Not scored' when CVSS data is null on both list and detail views. Never display 0.0/10 for missing data.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null; consistency.md > Data Formatting

findings.observation: Every patch shows 0 affected endpoints. This is a null value displayed as zero — the system has not performed endpoint-to-patch matching, not determined zero matches. Showing 0 implies 'assessed, nothing affected' when it means 'not assessed'.
findings.suggestion: Show '--' for patches where endpoint matching has not been performed. Reserve '0' for genuinely matched patches with zero affected endpoints.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null; standards-overrides > Missing numeric: -- not 0

findings.observation: Selecting Windows, Ubuntu, RHEL, or Debian from OS Family filter returns 0 results for every option. The os_family field is not populated on any of 207,222 patches. A primary filter that returns nothing for every option is functionally broken.
findings.suggestion: Populate os_family during catalog ingestion/normalization. If not yet mapped, remove the filter rather than presenting a broken feature.

findings.observation: Critical (30,706) + High (79,657) + Medium (92,484) + Low (4,371) = 207,218, but TOTAL shows 207,222. Off by 4 patches. Numbers that don't add up on the same screen destroy trust immediately.
findings.suggestion: Investigate 4 missing patches (likely NULL severity). Either add 'Unclassified' bucket or ensure all patches have valid severity.

findings.element: CATEGORY column — empty for all rows
findings.observation: Category column is blank for every row in the table. Detail page shows 'Security' for the same patch, proving data exists but is not rendered in list view. Empty cells should show '--', never blank space.
findings.suggestion: Render category data in list table from the same source as detail page. If null, show '--'.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; consistency.md > Data Formatting

findings.element: Stat cards — stale when filters applied
findings.observation: When severity/search/OS filters are active, TOTAL updates but CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW cards retain global counts. Search 'Dawn' shows TOTAL=1 but Critical=30,706. The numbers create a mathematical contradiction on screen.
findings.suggestion: All stat cards must reflect the filtered dataset, or clearly indicate they show global totals vs filtered counts.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals; consistency.md > Cross-element consistency

findings.element: Stat cards + severity pills — duplicate filtering
findings.observation: Two UI mechanisms filter by the same dimension: 5 clickable stat cards AND 5 severity pills with counts. Both filter identically, waste vertical space, and confuse users about which is authoritative.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate into one mechanism. Make stat cards non-interactive summaries or remove the pills entirely.
findings.standard_violated: consistency.md > Action Patterns; visual.md > Visual Weight

findings.element: Export button — permanently disabled, no tooltip
findings.observation: Export button is grayed out with no tooltip explaining why. Enterprise buyers need CSV export for compliance reporting. A disabled feature with no explanation signals incomplete product.
findings.suggestion: Implement CSV export or add tooltip 'Export coming soon'. Consider hiding entirely if not implemented.
findings.standard_violated: interaction.md > Disabled States; enterprise.md > Print and Export

findings.observation: Breadcrumb shows 'Patches / f12a5184-38d1-4e2e-b906-fe9...' — a truncated UUID. Enterprise users should never see internal database identifiers.
findings.suggestion: Replace UUID in breadcrumb with the patch name.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > No Debug Artifacts; consistency.md > Navigation Patterns

findings.observation: Summary bar shows 'DEPLOYED 0 (0%)' when affected endpoints is also 0. 0/0 is undefined, not 0%. Misleading precision with no denominator context.
findings.suggestion: Show '--' or 'N/A' when affected endpoints = 0 or null.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Percentages; data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null

findings.element: Remediation Metrics tab — charts with contradictory data
findings.observation: Shows bar charts with data despite 0 affected endpoints, 0 CVEs, 0 deployments. Other tabs say 'No data' but Remediation shows charts. Data contradicts summary bar above.
findings.suggestion: Show empty state when no deployments exist. Do not render charts with placeholder data.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Charts must be honest; consistency.md > Data sourcing

findings.observation: 99.99% of patches are 'Pending'. 'Pending' is not in the canonical status list (available/approved/installed/failed/superseded). Status lifecycle is non-functional.
findings.suggestion: Use 'Available' as default status for ingested patches. Implement full status lifecycle.

findings.element: Detail page title — CVE ID not patch name
findings.observation: Detail page h1 shows 'CVE-2026-0686' (a CVE ID) as the patch name. Same data identity problem as PO-001 but on the detail page.
findings.suggestion: Display actual patch name as h1. Show CVE association separately.

findings.observation: Empty search results show only plain text 'No patches found.' — no icon, no context, no CTA. Should use EmptyState component with search icon, headline, subtext, and 'Clear filters' action.
findings.suggestion: Use EmptyState component: icon + 'No patches match your filters' + 'Try adjusting your search' + Clear Filters CTA.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; consistency-overrides > Empty Table State

findings.observation: Expand chevrons, view toggles, and row action buttons have no aria-labels. Screen readers hear 'button' with no indication of purpose.
findings.suggestion: Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons: 'Expand row', 'Table view', 'Grid view', 'Create deployment for [name]'.
findings.standard_violated: accessibility.md > Links and Buttons; accessibility.md > Images and Icons

findings.observation: Search input, OS Family select, Status select lack <label> elements. Placeholder text is not a label. Console warns about 3 form fields without id/name attributes.
findings.suggestion: Add visually-hidden <label> elements or aria-label attributes to all form inputs.

findings.element: No kebab menu for row actions
findings.observation: Rightmost column shows only 'Create Deployment' icon button. Standard requires kebab menu with multiple row actions (View Details, Deploy, Mark Reviewed, etc.).
findings.suggestion: Replace single icon button with kebab menu containing contextual row actions.

findings.element: No bulk selection or bulk actions
findings.observation: No checkbox column, no 'Select' button, no bulk action toolbar. IT admins managing 207K patches need to select and deploy in bulk — not one at a time.
findings.suggestion: Implement selection mode: 'Select' button activates checkboxes, bulk action toolbar with 'Deploy Selected'.
findings.standard_violated: consistency-overrides > Selection Mode; end-user > Bulk operations at scale

findings.observation: All 207,222 patches show '1d ago'. Real catalogs span years. Data was bulk-imported with catalog sync timestamp, not actual vendor publication dates.
findings.suggestion: Preserve original publication dates from upstream feeds during ingestion.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Dates and Times; enterprise.md > Professional Appearance

findings.element: Expanded row — empty data, all zeros
findings.observation: Expanded row shows ENDPOINT EXPOSURE all zeros, CVE LINKS 'none'. Every expanded row looks identical — no useful differentiation. Feature is functionally useless with current data.
findings.suggestion: Show meaningful empty state when enrichment data unavailable: 'Run a discovery scan to populate endpoint exposure.'
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null; enterprise.md > Performance Perception

findings.observation: No indication of when the patch catalog was last synchronized. Admins need to know if data is current or stale.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Catalog last synced: 2h ago' indicator near page header with 'Sync Now' action.

findings.observation: Sorting by Severity sorts alphabetically (Critical, High, Low, Medium) instead of semantic order (Critical > High > Medium > Low).
findings.suggestion: Implement custom sort comparator: Critical=1, High=2, Medium=3, Low=4.

findings.observation: 'Patches' is rendered as StaticText in banner, not as h1. Violates heading hierarchy and accessibility standards.
findings.suggestion: Wrap 'Patches' page title in an <h1> element.

findings.observation: When filters are active, no 'Clear all' or 'Reset' action visible. User must manually clear each filter individually.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Clear filters' button that appears when any filter is active.
findings.standard_violated: interaction-overrides > Filtering > Clear all filters action

findings.element: Grid view total differs from table view
findings.observation: Table view shows 207,222 total; grid view shows 207,221. Off-by-one between views of the same data is a data consistency bug.
findings.suggestion: Both views must use the same count query/endpoint.
findings.standard_violated: consistency.md > Data Formatting; data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals

findings.observation: Pagination shows page numbers but no option to change rows per page (15/25/50/100). With 207K patches, users need to control page density.
findings.suggestion: Add per-page selector dropdown (15 | 25 | 50 | 100).

findings.observation: When filters reduce results, pagination shows 'Showing 1-15 of 30,706' without indicating it is a filtered subset of 207,222.
findings.suggestion: Show 'Showing 1-15 of 30,706 patches (filtered from 207,222 total)' when filters active.

findings.element: Detail page empty tabs — minimal empty states
findings.observation: CVEs, Affected Endpoints, and Deployment History tabs show only plain text empty messages. No icon, no subtext, no CTA.
findings.suggestion: Use EmptyState component with relevant icon, contextual message, and action button.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; consistency-overrides > Empty Table State

findings.element: Grid view cards — minimal useful info
findings.observation: Grid cards show CVE ID, severity badge, and empty metrics (CVES --, CVSS --, AFFECTED 0). Cards add no value over table view. Card anatomy does not match standard (missing header/body/footer structure).
findings.suggestion: Improve card anatomy: patch name header + severity badge, key metrics body, actions footer. Hide empty metric fields.

findings.observation: Stat cards show value at top and label below, with inconsistent border treatments (TOTAL has accent border, CRITICAL has red). Standard specifies label-top then value-below.
findings.suggestion: Standardize stat card anatomy: label top, value below. Consistent border treatment across all cards.

findings.observation: At 768x1024, stat cards extremely compressed, filter bar controls cramped. No 'best viewed on desktop' message per responsive standard.
findings.suggestion: Add responsive message below 1024px. Consider stacking stat cards vertically at narrow widths.

findings.element: Hover states — subtle and hard to distinguish
findings.observation: Stat card and row button hover changes are barely perceptible. Standard requires border-hover color change and translateY(-1px) lift.
findings.suggestion: Increase hover contrast: visible border lightening and lift effect.
findings.standard_violated: interaction.md > Hover States; interaction-overrides > Stat Card Hover

findings.observation: Accessibility 86 (below 90 target), SEO 60 (very low). Missing meta tags, heading structure issues, 50 unlabeled buttons contribute.
findings.suggestion: Fix all accessibility findings to reach 95+. Add meta tags for SEO.

findings.element: Published column — relative time without absolute tooltip
findings.observation: All rows show '1d ago' with no tooltip showing absolute date. Standard requires absolute time in tooltip for relative displays.
findings.suggestion: Add tooltip on hover showing absolute date (e.g., 'Apr 3, 2026 at 2:34 PM').

findings.element: Console warning — form fields without id/name
findings.observation: Console reports 'A form field element should have an id or name attribute (count: 3)'. Affects form submission and accessibility.
findings.suggestion: Add id and name attributes to search input, OS Family dropdown, and Status dropdown.

findings.observation: Search input has no X button to clear text. Users must select and delete manually.
findings.suggestion: Add clear (X) icon button inside search input when text is present.

findings.element: Deployment wizard — no patch context from header button
findings.observation: Header 'Create Deployment' opens wizard without indicating context. Row-level button pre-selects patch, but header button is generic.
findings.suggestion: Optionally pre-filter or indicate context: 'Creating deployment from Patches view.'

findings.observation: Detail page 'Mark Reviewed' button has no tooltip explaining what 'reviewed' means or what happens when clicked.
findings.suggestion: Add tooltip: 'Mark as reviewed by your security team. Reviewed patches are tracked in audit log.'

findings.observation: Severity values use colored text only (Critical in red, High in orange). Universal badge pattern requires 6px colored dot + text.
findings.suggestion: Add colored dot before severity text to match status badge pattern.

findings.observation: Last page number '13815' lacks thousands separator. Should show '13,815'.
findings.suggestion: Format page numbers with thousands separators.
